name: SignalBrief Social Intel
description:
Social mention intelligence for any brand, competitor, or keyword. Aggregates real-time mentions from Hacker News and X (Twitter) into a single structured brief with sentiment analysis, theme clustering, and urgency-ranked highlights.

Features:
- Aggregates mentions from Hacker News stories/comments and X (Twitter) tweets
- Engagement-weighted sentiment analysis (positive, neutral, negative)
- Theme clustering: pricing_complaints, support_issues, feature_requests, switching_intent, praise, general_discussion
- Urgency scoring (0-10) combining sentiment, churn language, and engagement signals
- Top 5 most important mentions with direct source links
- Actionable recommendation based on dominant themes
- Configurable time windows: 24h, 7d, 30d

Try asking:
- "What are people saying about Stripe on Hacker News and Twitter this week?"
- "Get me a social brief for Notion over the last 24 hours"
- "Compare sentiment around Figma vs Canva in the last 7 days"
- "Are there any urgent mentions about our brand Linear in the last 30 days?"
- "Show me switching intent signals for Slack from the past week"
- "What feature requests are people making about Vercel on social media?"
- "Give me a full social intelligence brief for OpenAI with urgency-ranked mentions"

Agent tips:
- Use the 7d window (default) for a balanced view; 24h for breaking issues; 30d for trend analysis
- The tool returns both human-readable text (summary + recommendation) and full structured data
- X/Twitter data requires a configured bearer token; the tool gracefully falls back to HN-only if unavailable
- Rate limited: max 10 requests/minute due to upstream API constraints
